message: 🌱 Add keycloak: External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for mission (#738)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-14644-external-idp-tokens-are-not-refreshed-automatically-for-oauth2-oi",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for OAuth2 & OIDC IDPs when retrieving the external token",
+    "description": "External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for OAuth2 & OIDC IDPs when retrieving the external token. Requested by 62+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current keycloak deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your keycloak version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak\nhelm list -n keycloak 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working keycloak installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review keycloak configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant keycloak configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak\nkubectl get configmap -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keycloak\n```\n### Describe the bug\n\nWe're using Github (specifically a Github app) as an external IDP. We have configured Github to use expiring access tokens (good for 8 hours)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for",
+        "description": "I think, this is only explaining that using the refresh token outside of Keycloak is no viable workaround.\n\"If the refresh token is still good, it is worthless because using it will expire the one held in Keycloak.\" \n\nWhen Keycloak refreshes the access token in time, the app will never need the refresh token as assumed by .\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak\nkubectl get events -n keycloak --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I think, this is only explaining that using the refresh token outside of Keycloak is no viable workaround.\n\"If the refresh token is still good, it is worthless because using it will expire the one held in Keycloak.\" \n\nWhen Keycloak refreshes the access token in time, the app will never need the refresh token as assumed by .",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "community",

+      "issue": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/14644",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak"
+    },
+    "reactions": 62,
+    "comments": 41,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with keycloak installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
